CAMPAIGN
Preterm Birth Awareness
Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health
San Francisco Department of Public Health (SFDPH) Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health Office (MCAH) is committed to helping prevent preterm birth and infant death. MCAH is connecting people with resources to prevent preterm birth.
Preterm birth is the #1 cause of infant death
Over 500 babies are born preterm in San Francisco each year
Preterm care costs 10x more than the average hospital cost in California
Call 800-300-9950 (SFDPH MCAH Office) if you know anyone at risk or pregnant with these risk factors
Over 90% of
people who had a preterm birth in San Francisco during 2023 to 2025 had
two or more of these risk factors before 37 weeks of pregnancy:
3 or more
children
Age over 35
Previous preterm birth
Fetal loss or miscarriage
Address in
zip code
94124, 94133, 94115, 94103, 94102, 94112, 94134, 94158, 94132, or 94105
Public health insurance or low income
Homeless or address in public
housing
, SRO, or shelter
Physically demanding
work
(like lifting, bending, standing, security)
Nutrition issue
Smoking or
substance use
Asthma
High
blood pressure (>130/80)
High
blood sugar
Infection
Ultrasound showing poor fetal growth
Referral to Resources
MCAH can refer to agencies in San Francisco that offer resources
to prevent preterm birth including: Prenatal Care • Food Services
Housing • Hospital Care • Doula Care • Public Health Nurse
Health Insurance • Social Services • Career Services • Mental Health
Substance Counseling • Safe Water, Housing, Air, Soil • Work
Accommodations • Transportation • Education
